input_dict.agent_role: Integrate second-hop evidence with first-hop context and retain the facts needed to answer the question.
input_dict.question: What do the Lost McLeod Mine and the Colomac Mine have in common?
input_dict.current_output: Both sites are in the Northwest Territories, Canada. Colomac was a privately owned open‑pit gold mine (context); the Lost McLeod Mine is a legendary lost mine in the Northwest Territories ("Lost McLeod Mine is a legend of a lost mine"), and the retrieved passages do not specify its commodity, so it is unclear from these sources whether it was a gold mine (lost‑mine legends "usually" involve gold).

upstream_context.question: What do the Lost McLeod Mine and the Colomac Mine have in common?
upstream_context.summary_1: Both passages place the sites in the Northwest Territories, Canada: "Lost McLeod Mine is a legend of a lost mine in the Northwest Territories of Canada" (passage 1) and "The Colomac Mine ... was a privately owned and operated open pit gold mine located 220 km northwest of Yellowknife in the Northwest Territories in Canada" (passage 2). Missing/ambiguous: passage 1 does not say what commodity (e.g., gold) the Lost McLeod Mine involved. Next hop: retrieve sources that state the mineral/commodity associated with the Lost McLeod Mine (or authoritative mentions linking it to gold) to determine whether they share being gold mines beyond their shared Northwest Territories location.
upstream_context.hop2_query: Lost McLeod Mine gold Northwest Territories legend
hop2_docs: Lost McLeod Mine | Lost McLeod Mine is a legend of a lost mine in the Northwest Territories of Canada.  The story has been featured in many books and magazines.  The events in the legend have led to geographic locations in the Northwest Territories being named Headless Valley and Headless Creek located in Nahanni National Park Reserve.  A creek called Sheep Creek was renamed McCleod Creek in honour of the Mcleod Brothers.  20 people have lost their lives searching for The Lost McLeod Mine.

Pitt Lake's lost gold mine | Pitt Lake's Lost Gold Mine is a legendary lost mine said to be near Pitt Lake, British Columbia, Canada, the supposed wealth of which has held the imagination of people worldwide for more than a century.  Ever since the years of the Fraser Canyon Gold Rush prospectors and adventurers have been looking for the mine and gold-rush rumors have evolved into legends repeated and enriched over time.  The mysterious riches are known as Slumach’s Lost Mine, or Lost Creek Mine.

List of lost mines | Lost mines are a popular form of lost treasure legend.  The mine involved is usually of a high-value commodity such as gold, silver or diamonds.  Often there is a map (sometimes called a "waybill") purportedly showing the location of the mine.  Common reasons given for the mines being lost include:

Lost Dutchman's Gold Mine | The Lost Dutchman's Gold Mine (also known by similar names) is, according to legend, a rich gold mine hidden in the southwestern United States.  The location is generally believed to be in the Superstition Mountains, near Apache Junction, east of Phoenix, Arizona.  There have been many stories about how to find the mine, and each year people search for the mine.  Some have died on the search.

Ruth Mine | The Ruth Mine was a small gold producer about 65 km (40 miles) east of Yellowknife, Northwest Territories.  It operated briefly during 1942 and then again in 1959, extracting only 550 troy ounces (17 kg) of gold thus far.  The old mine is today (2012) significant as one of the last intact abandoned mining properties from the 1940s period in the Northwest Territories, although the site was scheduled to be remediated by the federal government.

Courageous Lake mine | The Courageous Lake mine is one of the largest gold mines in Canada and in the world.  The mine is located in the north of the country in Northwest Territories.  The mine has estimated reserves of 11.4 million oz of gold.

Quartz Mountain mine | The Quartz Mountain mine is one of the largest gold mines in Canada and in the world.  The mine is located in the north of the country in Northwest Territories.  The mine has estimated reserves of 2.74 million oz of gold.

visible_gold_support.title: Lost McLeod Mine
visible_gold_support.sentence: Lost McLeod Mine is a legend of a lost mine in the Northwest Territories of Canada.
